_Captured Thursday, 26 September 2024 12:13 pm · Computational › Other Tools & CAD_

- Creating an environment (Never work in Base)
- Activate an environment (Move you out of base into that)
- Install packages
- Launch JupyterLab
- Deactivate environment
- Creaating an environment
- To load terminal; Anaconda prompt
- To check the version; Conda --version
- To list ENV: conda env list
- To create new environment; conda create --name"Example"
- To activate: conda activate "Example"
- To list package; conda list
- To install package; conda install "package name"
- To install package from conda-forge: conda install -c conda-forge condastats
- To run jupyter-lab; jupyter-lab
- To end jupyter-lab: ctrl+c
- To deactivate env: conda deactivate
- What is IDE?
- Integrated Development Environment
- Text editor
- Integrated build tools (Programming language)
- Debugger
- Example IDE
- PiChart
- Spider is similar to mathlab
- Jupyter
- Conda install ipykernel
- conda install notebook
- Python -m ipykernel install --user --name=test (Start new environment as soon as lunch the notebook)
- Lunch jupyter notebook, jupyter notebook
- Open the environment : New pink ones
- Spyder
- Conda install spyder-kernels
- Change environment: bottom bar/python intepreter

_Captured Tuesday, 31 December 2024 11:24 am · Computational › Other Tools & CAD_


**Reference documents**
- BIM Execution Plan
- WSP NZ - BIM Execution Plan Template
- A BIM Execution Plan (BEP) provides a foundation to ensure deployment and delivery of a project is successful. It outlines the project specific approach to digital implementation including:
- Digital authoring tools of choice
- Project goals and objectives
- Project specific standards and guidelines
- Project coordinate system and base points
- Level of Development
- Applicable BIM Uses
- Project team roles and responsibilities
- Exchange Information Requirements
- A comprehensive version of the WSP BIM Execution Plan template is available and ready for use.
- WSP BIM Roles & Responsibilities
- This is a general rule of thumb breakdown and a blend of roles may be most suited to a WSP project.
- Drawing Mark-up Protocol
- WSP NZ - Project - Drawing Markup Cheat Sheet
- The draughtsperson is to carry out a self-check to ensure that what has been drawn/detailed & compared against current drawings does not include any major or minor issues i.e. duplicated section/elevation marks, clarity of drawings, consistency etc. Once complete, send a dated copy to the engineer and a drawing verifier for checking. For ease of communicating the markups, use the provided document as a guide.
- Level of Development (LOD)
- BIM+Han

_Captured Wednesday, 1 January 2025 11:39 pm · Computational › Other Tools & CAD_

- Digital Delivery Onboarding
- This document provides useful links and guidance for both new and established teams in the digital design and delivery space.
- WSP NZ - Digital Onboarding
- Draughting
- 2D CAD Drawings and Xrefs
- For all 2D CAD drawings (i.e. drawings with no Civil 3D elements) and Xrefs please use the 2D CAD template. It contains all the styles and line types that are normally used. A template available from drawings set up in metres and millimetres. Path to the templates as shown below:
- `C:\Users\ausfd0\OneDrive - WSP O365\5- DesignAid\Civil 3D\WSP - Q Drive\AutoCAD\01_Drawing Templates`
- WSP CAD Standards
- Refer to the document below for the current WSP CAD standards. A water sector specific CAD standards is currently under development. A draft version will be made available in due course.
- T-CSP 404_WSP NZ Drawing Presentation Standard
- T-CSP 404a_WSP NZ Drawing Presentation Pictorial Supplement

**Diagrams**
![CAD font size](https://sattarfd.github.io/image-host/sof_196_cad-font-size_1.png)

- 3.5 in 1:1 scale is 3.5mm as given in word (convert that to inch and multiply by 72 to get as point given in word software
- 3.5/25=0.14 inch
- 0.14*72=10.08 Point
- 0.08*72=5.76 inch
- 1.5/25=0.06 inch
- 0.06*72=4.32 inch

- To create boundary line - Concave Hull
- Export as *.pts file form recap
- QGIS
- In
- Layer > Add Layer > Add Delimited Text Layer
- .
- Geometry deffinition> define the which
- fields are the X and Y coordinates
- .
- Set CRS: in search type 2193>
- In processing toolbox search for Concave Hull
- Uncheck the allow holes
- Set the threshold to 0.03
- Output
- Right click on layer> Save feature as
- Format > autodesk dxf
- Geometry type> Polygon
- Import into CAD
- To get poly line
- HATCHGENERATEBOUNDARY

**To have points in actual colour**
- 2. Save It as a Real Vector Layer
- Right-click
- the new layer →
- Export > Save Features As

**In the export dialog**
- Format
- : Choose GeoPackage (recommended) or ESRI Shapefile
- File name
- : e.g., colored_points.gpkg
- ✅
- CRS
- : Set to EPSG:2193 (NZTM2000)
- Leave others default
- Click
- OK
- 3. Add the Saved Layer
- QGIS may automatically load it after export.
- If not, use
- Layer > Add Layer > Add Vector Layer
- and browse to
